name: Image to UI Code
description: Deconstruct a UI screenshot into parts, spawn parallel subagents to annotate each subcomponent's styling and layout, and generate clean, responsive UI code.
triggers: image to direct ui ux code

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.description: Path to the UI image or screenshot file.

inputs.name: target_framework
inputs.description: Target UI framework: html/css (default), react/tailwind, nextjs/scss.

outputs.name: component_annotations
outputs.description: JSON/YAML hierarchy detailing sections and components extracted by subagents.

outputs.name: generated_code
outputs.description: The responsive markup and styling matching the UI image.

gates.rule: Verify vision capabilities or image path availability.
Confirm code structure does not contain placeholders or AI slop patterns.
Perform visual hierarchy verification and anti-slop quality check.

steps.description: Delegate screenshot analysis to parallel subagents to annotate layout coordinates and structure for header, sidebar, grid, and detail components.
Assemble annotations and specify visual hierarchy mapping inputs, buttons, text nodes, and container constraints.
Resolve typography rules, responsive breakpoints, borders, and colors to map visual fidelity.
Synthesize markup structures and bundle CSS classes according to target_framework specification.
Audit visual hierarchy, color contrast, and semantic markup for WCAG Level AA compatibility.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Save generated component files and code stylesheets.

verification.method: Ensure generated component passes typescript or html validation.
Verify code contains zero lorem ipsum, mock comments, or unstyled blocks.

success_criteria: Generated code matches 1:1 visual structure of the screenshot.
Responsive styling and layout break rules are applied.
Accessibility contrast and aria attributes are included.

rollback.when: If code validation fails syntax check, revert generated changes and log error.
